ev.headline: BYD Outsells Tesla in Europe for Second Consecutive Month
ev.summary: BYD registered 17,954 vehicles compared to Tesla's 17,664 in February 2026, signaling a significant shift in European EV market dynamics.

ev.headline: Toyota Expanding EV Portfolio Amid Industry Pullback
ev.summary: Toyota announced its first American-made battery-electric vehicle with the Highlander EV launching later in 2026, bringing total BEV offerings to six.

ev.headline: EV Consideration Climbs as Gas Prices Surge Amid Iran Conflict
ev.summary: Electrified vehicle research activity reached 23.8% in mid-March, the highest level this year, as consumers reassess EVs in response to soaring gasoline prices.

ev.headline: Tesla Semi Reveals 1,000 lbs Weight Savings Achieving Diesel Parity
ev.summary: Jay Leno toured Tesla's production-intent electric truck, revealing engineering details including weight savings that bring the 500-mile version to payload parity with diesel trucks.

ev.headline: Chery Unveils Solid-State Battery Achieving 1,500 km Range
ev.summary: Chery's new solid-state battery with 600 Wh/kg energy density targets 1,500 km driving range, with vehicle testing planned for next year.

ev.headline: BAIC Announces Sodium-Ion Battery Breakthrough with 11-Minute Charging
ev.summary: Beijing Automotive Group revealed sodium-ion battery prototype with 4C ultra-fast charging, fully recharging in approximately 11 minutes.

ev.headline: China Controls 70% of Global EV Battery Supply End of 2025
ev.summary: Chinese battery manufacturers dominated with 70% market share, with global EV battery installations reaching 1,187 GWh, up 31.7% from 2024.

ev.headline: Lamborghini Shelves All-Electric Lanzador Due to Charging Infrastructure Concerns
ev.summary: Lamborghini canceled plans for its 1,341-horsepower electric Ultra GT, citing poor charging infrastructure and lack of customer demand, pivoting instead to plug-in hybrids.

ev.headline: Rivian, Uber Partner on 50,000 Autonomous Robotaxis Deployment
ev.summary: A $1.25 billion deal will bring Rivian's R2 SUV to Uber's autonomous network starting in 2028, with plans to deploy up to 50,000 vehicles across 25 cities by 2031.

ev.headline: EV Charging Shifts from Quantity to Reliability in 2026
ev.summary: Advanced monitoring systems and real-time availability integration into vehicle navigation are replacing focus on charger quantity, with multi-charger stations reducing wait times.

ev.headline: EVgo Plans 500+ NACS Connectors by End of 2026
ev.summary: After a successful 2025 pilot installing nearly 100 NACS connectors across 22 cities, EVgo plans to accelerate deployment to support rising NACS-equipped EV demand.

ev.headline: Ford Takes $19.5 Billion Charge, Shifts to Hybrids and ICE Vehicles
ev.summary: Ford announced a major strategic shift away from pure EVs toward hybrids and internal combustion engines, with plans to focus on smaller, affordable electric models.

ev.headline: GM Records $6 Billion EV Writedown in January 2026
ev.summary: General Motors announced a significant $6 billion writedown as part of a $7.6 billion total impact from scaling back aggressive EV investments.

ev.headline: Federal NEVI Program Faces $500 Million Budget Cut
ev.summary: The National Electric Vehicle Infrastructure program is facing a $500 million reduction as part of proposed $900 million in EV-related transportation cuts.

ev.headline: States Drive EV Transition Amid Federal Policy Uncertainty
ev.summary: All 50 states have adopted multiple EV policies, with initiatives ranging from financial incentives to charging infrastructure expansion, proving state-level leadership in electrification.

ev.headline: New EV Sales Down 26.8% Year-Over-Year Through February 2026
ev.summary: New EV sales declined 26.8% compared to February 2025, though used EV sales continued climbing as off-lease inventory floods the market.

ev.headline: Over 20 New EV Models Launching in 2026
ev.summary: Nearly 30 new electric models are slated for the U.S. market in 2026, representing first-generation models from major automakers and new mass-market offerings from startups.

ev.headline: Lexus Expands EV Lineup with New ES Plug-In Hybrid Variants
ev.summary: Lexus introduced new front-wheel-drive ES 350e and all-wheel-drive ES 500e plug-in hybrid models, expanding its electrified vehicle portfolio.

ev.headline: Tesla Faces Expanded Federal Probe Over Self-Driving Performance
ev.summary: Federal safety regulators raised concerns about Tesla's automated driving-assistance system, particularly its camera-only approach in poor weather conditions.

ev.headline: Mercedes-Benz Launches All-Electric VLE Grand Limousine
ev.summary: Mercedes introduced the eight-seat all-electric VLE featuring 800-volt charging, over 700 km WLTP range, and flexible interior on new Van Architecture.

ev.headline: Global EV Charging Infrastructure Market Projected at $382.83 Billion by 2035
ev.summary: Global EV charging infrastructure market is valued at $40.86 billion in 2025 and is predicted to reach $382.83 billion by 2035 at 25.2% CAGR.

ev.headline: Renault Announces futuREady Plan Targeting Two Million Vehicle Sales by 2030
ev.summary: Renault's futuREady plan anchors on new 800V electric platform, extended hybrid commitment, and expansion across five international hubs to reach two million sales.

ev.headline: EV Battery Repair Costs Top €12,500 in Europe as Complexity Increases
ev.summary: Inverter repair costs average €3,450 with prices ranging from €900 to €6,000 per vehicle, reflecting rising EV maintenance complexity across Europe.

energy.headline: Australia's rooftop solar hits 28.3 GW as home battery uptake surges
energy.summary: Australian homes installed 2.6 GW of new rooftop solar in 2025 and battery installations more than doubled with 183,245 units installed in H2 2025, driven by the $7.3 billion government home battery program.

energy.headline: Home battery demand hits record in Australia as rooftop solar rebounds
energy.summary: February 2026 saw 281 MW of new rooftop solar installed in Australia, the largest February total on record, while battery adoption continues its explosive growth.

energy.headline: Copper's Battery-Equipped Induction Range Makes Electrification & Storage Accessible
energy.summary: Copper launches an induction range with integrated battery storage at $5,999, enabling home electrification for older buildings with limited electrical capacity and reducing upgrade costs.

energy.headline: Base Power to launch 100-MW home battery network for Texas utility
energy.summary: Base Power will install 100 megawatts of residential battery capacity over two years for Denton County Electric Cooperative, equivalent to a gas-fired peaker plant's output.

energy.headline: Vermont utility makes it easier than ever to add a home backup battery
energy.summary: Green Mountain Power offers home batteries via affordable lease or lump-sum payment ($55/month or $5,500 one-time), leveraging tax credit loopholes to provide budget-friendly storage solutions.

energy.headline: Sunrun's Latest Residential Solar Battery Innovations Reshape US Home Energy Markets in 2026
energy.summary: Sunrun launches AI-optimized solar-plus-storage systems cutting energy costs up to 40%, with 183,245 battery units projected and lifetime customer value of $80,000.

energy.headline: U.S. Adds 58 GWh of New Energy Storage Capacity in 2025, Largest Single Year on Record
energy.summary: The U.S. installed a record 57.6 GWh of battery storage in 2025, with residential storage up 51% year-over-year, driven by virtual power plant programs and grid resilience needs.

energy.headline: Net metering for solar owners suffers defeat in California courts
energy.summary: A California appeals court upheld reduced net metering compensation rates, siding with utilities on lower solar export credits, which may slow residential adoption.

energy.headline: Tax credits for solar panels are available, but the catch is you can't own them
energy.summary: Solar installers shift toward leases and power purchase agreements after homeowner tax credits expired, as third-party ownership remains eligible for 30% federal incentive through 2028.

energy.headline: How to Make Solar Work for You in 2026
energy.summary: Post-tax-credit solar in 2026 requires focus on net metering policies, utility rates, battery integration, and long-term energy strategy beyond incentive-driven purchases.

energy.headline: Solar installs surged 205% before the tax credit cut – and reshaped the market
energy.summary: U.S. homeowners rushed to install solar before December 31, 2025 deadline, driving 205% year-over-year surge, with market now shifting to whole-home electrification systems.

energy.headline: Senator mocked 'green energy crap.' His house runs on it.
energy.summary: Montana Senator Tim Sheehy, who criticized solar subsidies, installed rooftop panels and battery storage at his home using the 26% federal tax credit before voting against renewables.

energy.headline: Federal Solar Tax Credit (ITC): Ending January 1, 2026
energy.summary: The 30% federal residential solar tax credit expired on December 31, 2025, ending a two-decade incentive era unless homeowners choose third-party ownership models.

energy.headline: Treasury and IRS Release Interim Guidance on Foreign Entity Restrictions for Solar Tax Credits
energy.summary: New FEOC rules set 40% non-foreign content threshold for 2026 solar installations to qualify for tax credits, with stricter 55% requirement for energy storage systems.

energy.headline: Net metering policies see significant revision in states across the U.S.
energy.summary: One-third of U.S. states are revising net metering policies, with major actions in Nevada, Idaho, Maryland, and Texas shifting away from full retail-rate compensation.

energy.headline: Experts predict the top trends in U.S. residential solar for 2026
energy.summary: Solar costs at $2.48/watt reflect record lows, with market shifting to prepaid third-party ownership (TPO) models, AI-driven optimization, and whole-home electrification strategies.

energy.headline: Colorado Solar Incentives 2026 – Rebates, Tax Exemptions, Net Metering & Savings
energy.summary: Colorado maintains net metering with retail-rate credits, property tax exemptions, and utility rebates in 2026, though the federal 30% tax credit has expired.

energy.headline: 10 solar, storage and energy predictions for 2026
energy.summary: Battery storage costs declining to $108/kWh enable cost-effective multi-day blackout protection, while data center demand drives growth in utility-scale storage deployment.

energy.headline: Charge Home Solutions Expands Tesla Powerwall Installation Nationwide in the U.S.
energy.summary: Nationwide Tesla Powerwall installation expansion addresses rising demand for home battery backup amid increasing electricity costs and grid reliability concerns.

energy.headline: Virginia Net Metering Changes: The End of Solar as We Know It?
energy.summary: Dominion Energy and Appalachian Power seek Virginia net metering rule changes that could reduce solar payback periods from 9-15 years, with early adopters grandfathered in.

energy.headline: Missed the 30% Tax Credit? How to Make Solar Work for You in 2026
energy.summary: Post-2026 solar requires focus on long-term utility rates, battery integration, EV chargers, and smart panels rather than tax incentives for financial viability.

energy.headline: The Unexpected Clean Energy Winner of 2025: Energy Storage
energy.summary: Energy storage projects retained favorable tax credits through 2032 despite solar phase-out, driving record 57.6 GWh deployment and attracting data center demand.

energy.headline: Available Solar and Home Battery Incentives in 2026
energy.summary: Homeowners can access state rebates, utility incentives, grid services payments, and leasing options after federal residential tax credits expired at year-end 2025.

energy.headline: Is Solar Still Worth It After 2026
energy.summary: California analysis shows solar payback extends from 4.3 to 6.1 years without federal tax credit, but remains compelling at under 7 years for high-cost states.

energy.headline: UVA research could unlock better energy storage for solar power
energy.summary: University of Virginia researchers develop flow battery technology using redox-active materials to enable better long-duration energy storage for residential solar systems.

energy.headline: How to Survive the 2026 Residential Solar Market
energy.summary: Solar industry shifts to third-party ownership, battery integration, virtual power plants, and operations/maintenance services as tax-credit-driven sales model becomes obsolete.

v2x.headline: Volkswagen Explores New Revenue Streams Through Vehicle-to-Grid Technology
v2x.summary: Volkswagen Commercial Vehicles and energy provider Enercity launched a V2G trial with 75 ID.Buzz models to seek new revenue sources after significant profit decline and target margin rebound in 2026.

v2x.headline: Nio Participates in Vehicle-to-Grid Demonstration Discharge with Over 100 EVs
v2x.summary: More than 100 Nio vehicles participated in a V2G discharge demonstration in Tianjin, with the company now operating 3,639 battery swap stations across China featuring grid regulation capabilities.

v2x.headline: Electric Cars Can Make Power Grids More Reliable (and Earn Owners Money) – So Why Aren't We Doing That?
v2x.summary: A new study identifies barriers to V2G adoption in the U.S., revealing that coordination challenges among utilities, EV owners, and infrastructure providers rather than technical limitations are the primary obstacles.

v2x.headline: Grid Down? Scientists Say Your EV Could Provide the Needed Backup Power
v2x.summary: A Utilities Policy study outlines roadmaps for V2G adoption, noting that despite sounding ideal, V2G implementation barriers include coordination issues and inconsistent state-level policies affecting standardization efforts.

v2x.headline: Vehicle-to-Grid Market Set to Reach US$ 65.84 Billion by 2035
v2x.summary: The global V2G market is valued at $6.27 billion in 2025 and expected to reach $65.84 billion by 2035, growing at 26.50% CAGR, with ISO 15118-20 standardization forcing universal hardware interoperability in Europe.

v2x.headline: Yes, an EV really CAN power your home – if it's one of these [update]
v2x.summary: Tesla Model 3/Y owners can now achieve V2H charging via Sigenergy chargers, while the Cybertruck remains Tesla's only official bidirectional model; compatibility also extends to Kia EV6, Volvo EX30, and select Mercedes/VW vehicles.

v2x.headline: Top 10 EV Battery Trends in 2025 and What We Can Expect in 2026
v2x.summary: Machine learning-enhanced charging systems, thermal adaptive charging, and V2G integration are emerging trends for 2026, with battery prices dropping 8% and potential 50% cost reductions expected compared to 2023.

v2x.headline: Battery-Electric Vehicle-Charging Station-Power Grid Synergies: A Technical Roadmap from Vehicle to Grid
v2x.summary: A comprehensive technical review examines V2G systems for grid stability and renewable integration, highlighting battery degradation management, virtual power plant deployment challenges, and market-oriented coordination mechanisms as critical components.

v2x.headline: U.S. EV Battery Storage Projects Face Grid Interconnection Delays in 2026
v2x.summary: Utility-scale battery storage projects linked to EV infrastructure are experiencing extended approval timelines and transmission upgrade requirements as queue backlogs expand across U.S. regions.

v2x.headline: Two-Way Electric Vehicle Charging at Scale Could Stop Renewable Energy Being Wasted
v2x.summary: A UK bidirectional charging trial aims to address renewable curtailment; European power systems could save €4 billion through V2G, with over 100 global V2G test projects demonstrating diverse applications.

v2x.headline: 2026 EV Charging Industry Predictions and Trends
v2x.summary: V2G technology is poised to transform fleet and commercial energy management, with intelligent grid distribution and dynamic pricing aligned to utility tariffs as key enablers for 2026 deployments.

v2x.headline: GM leads the charge in vehicle-to-home EVs: inside the home energy ecosystem
v2x.summary: GM sold over 246,000 V2H-capable vehicles in 2025 and is exploring leasing programs for home energy systems to lower adoption barriers, positioning EVs as sources of backup power and grid support.

v2x.headline: Hyundai Motor Group Expands EV Energy Services with Vehicle to Grid and Vehicle to Home
v2x.summary: Hyundai Motor Group is accelerating V2X service rollout with V2G in Korea/Europe and V2H in the U.S., enabling EVs to store and supply electricity while reducing household costs and enhancing energy resilience.

v2x.headline: Vehicle-to-home charging technology launches for the mass market
v2x.summary: Six homes in California were outfitted with mass-market V2H technology using Kia EV9 vehicles and purpose-built bidirectional chargers, marking the first real-world deployment for consumer applications.

v2x.headline: Vehicle to Home (V2H) Charging Technology Arrives for Eligible Kia EV9 Drivers
v2x.summary: Kia EV9 owners can now access V2H capabilities through Wallbox Quasar 2 chargers, enabling backup power during outages and potential savings on electricity bills through peak-hour energy management.

v2x.headline: Nissan to launch affordable vehicle-to-grid technology in 2026
v2x.summary: Nissan announced it will launch affordable bidirectional charging on selected EVs in 2026, starting with AC-based systems in the UK that will be comparable in price to traditional chargers.

v2x.headline: 2026 Outlook on V2G Support for Energy Management Battery
v2x.summary: V2G has evolved from pilot projects to the backbone of battery aggregation, with Maryland's comprehensive V2G interconnection rules enabling the nation's first residential pilot; fleet operators are primary adopters post-tax credit expiration.

v2x.headline: What's next for EV batteries in 2026
v2x.summary: Sodium-ion batteries are entering vehicle production in China while LFP batteries are ramping in the U.S. for energy storage; global EV sales projected to reach 40% of new vehicles by 2030.

v2x.headline: Driving Energy Forward: An Introduction to Vehicle-to-Grid (V2G)
v2x.summary: V2G enables EVs to stabilize grids and serve as backup power; challenges include low adoption in some areas, lack of standardization across bidirectional chargers, and unclear compensation structures for grid services.

v2x.headline: What Is Vehicle-to-Grid (V2G) Technology and How Does It Work?
v2x.summary: V2G technology allows EVs to serve as backup storage and support grid stability using bidirectional charging; a single EV can power one home for 2-5 hours or five homes for approximately one hour.

v2x.headline: The Battery on Wheels Revolution And Why 2026 Marks the Dawn of the $5.8 Billion Vehicle-to-Grid Era
v2x.summary: 2026 is identified as the critical takeoff point for mass smart grid integration, with bidirectional EV charging projected to reach $5.8 billion by 2036 as EVs transform into distributed energy resources.

v2x.headline: What Is Bidirectional Charging? Understanding the Benefits for Both Drivers and Businesses
v2x.summary: Bidirectional chargers like Wallbox Quasar 2, dcbel r16, and Nuvve PowerPort enable V2H and V2G; Enphase and Emporia plan new product launches in 2026 to expand market options.
